LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RICHARD FERKO AND RICHARD T. FERKO, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO PLAINTIFF PAUL MCALEESE. PLAINTIFF MCALEESE HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS THAT HE IS THE REAL PARTY OF INTEREST TO SUE ON BEHALF OF PLAINTIFF CORPORATION 530 JACKSON STREET. (SEE FLADEBOE V. AMERICAN ISUZU MOTORS INC. (2007) 150 CAL.APP.4TH 42, 55.) PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS, IF PLAINTIFFS CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT AN AGREEMENT FOR LEGAL REPRESENTATION EXISTED BETWEEN DEFENDANT FERKO AND PLAINTIFF MCALEESE, AS AN INDIVIDUAL. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRERS ON THE GROUND THAT ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BARRED BY THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER THE EXHIBITS ATTACHED TO PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER IN RULING ON THIS DEMURRER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SUZANNE CROZIER, CSR # 4761 (302/MJM)
ORDER RE DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS RICHARD FERKO AND RICHARD T. FERKO, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO INDIVIDUAL PLAINTIFF MCALEESE. PLAINTIFF MCALEESE HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT HE IS THE REAL PARTY OF INTEREST TO SUE ON BEHALF OF CORPORATE PLAINTIFF 530 JACKSON STREET. (SEE FLADEBOE V. AMERICAN ISUZU MOTORS INC. (2007) 150 CAL.APP.4TH 42, 55.) DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ON THE GROUNDS THAT THEY ARE BARRED UNDER THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT IT DID NOT DISCOVER THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL CONDUCT UNTIL MAY 2012 (SAC 14), AND THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT WAS FILED ON FEBRUARY 14, 2013. THE FOUR-YEAR PROVISION UNDER CCP 340.6(A) IS TOLLED BECAUSE DEFENDANT CONTINUED TO REPRESENT PLAINTIFF 530 JACKSON STREET REGARDING THE UNDERLYING LITIGATION. (CCP 340.6(A); SAC 10-19.) DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED FACTS WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY TO MEET THE HEIGHTENED PLEADING STANDARD OF FRAUD. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ACCOUNTING IS OVERRULED AS PLAINTIFFS HAVE ALLEGED FACTS THAT A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP EXITS AND THAT PLAINTIFF NEVER RECEIVED AN ACCOUNTING OF THE SETTLEMENT MONEY THAT DEFENDANTS COLLECTED. (SAC 10, 13, 36.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-26-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Off Calendar Feb-24-2014 Continued To Apr-16-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Richard Ferko And Richard T. Ferko, A Professional Law Corporation'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TERENCE A . REDMOND, TEL. NO. 395-9000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
RICHARD T. FERKO, TEL. NO. (818) 712-2222, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Richard Ferko And Richard T. Ferko, A Professional Law Corporation's Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint - Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendants' demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to individual Plaintiff McAleese. Plaintiff McAleese has failed to allege facts that he is the real party of interest to sue on behalf of corporate Plaintiff 530 Jackson Street. (See Fladeboe v. American Isuzu Motors Inc. (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 42, 55.)
Defendants' demurrer to all causes of action on the grounds that they are barred under the applicable statute of limitations is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that it did not discover the alleged wrongful conduct until May 2012 (SAC 14), and the original complaint was filed on February 14, 2013. The four-year provision under CCP 340.6(a) is tolled because Defendant continued to represent Plaintiff 530 Jackson Street regarding the underlying litigation. (CCP 340.6(a); SAC 10-19.) Defendants' demurrer to the first and fourth causes of action is overruled as Plaintiff has alleged facts with sufficient particularity to meet the heightened pleading standard of fraud. Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action for accounting is overruled as Plaintiffs have alleged facts that a fiduciary relationship exits and that Plaintiff never received an accounting of the settlement money that Defendants collected. (SAC 10, 13, 36.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TERENCE A. REDMOND, TEL. NO. 395-9000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RICHARD FERKO, TEL. NO. (818) 712-2222 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS RICHARD FERKO and RICHARD T. FERKO, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Defendants' demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to Plaintiff Paul McAleese. Plaintiff McAleese has not alleged facts that he is the real party of interest to sue on behalf of Plaintiff corporation 530 Jackson Street. (See Fladeboe v. American Isuzu Motors Inc. (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 42, 55.) Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to allege facts, if Plaintiffs can do so in good faith, that an agreement for legal representation existed between Defendant Ferko and Plaintiff McAleese, as an individual.
Defendants' demurrers on the ground that all causes of action are barred by the applicable statute of limitations is sustained with leave to amend. The Court does not consider the exhibits attached to Plaintiffs' opposition to demurrer in ruling on this demurrer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Suzanne Crozier, CSR # 4761
=(302/MJM)
